mission.title: Install and Configure Flowise
mission.description: Flowise is an AI platform that allows users to build AI agents visually. It is ideal for developers looking to create and manage AI workflows without deep programming knowledge. Key trade-offs include the need for Node.js and Docker for installation and potential resource consumption depending on the complexity of the AI agents.

steps.title: Install the Flowise CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Flowise command-line interface globally on your machine, which is necessary to manage Flowise instances.

```bash

npm install -g flowise@3.0.13

```

> Install the specific version of Flowise globally.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create flowise-cluster --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1 --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials flowise-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Deploy Flowise on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys Flowise using a Helm chart, which simplifies the deployment process.

```bash

helm repo add flowise https://flowiseai.github.io/flowise

helm install flowise flowise/flowise --version 3.0.13 --namespace flowise --create-namespace

```

> Add the Flowise Helm repository.
> Install Flowise in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Verify Flowise Deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Flowise pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n flowise

```

> Look for the status of the Flowise pods to confirm they are running.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step includes setting up autoscaling and monitoring for the Flowise deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment flowise --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 -n flowise

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up horizontal pod autoscaler.
> Apply your monitoring configuration (ensure you have a monitoring setup ready).

uninstall.title: Uninstall Flowise
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the Flowise deployment and may result in data loss. Ensure to back up any important data before proceeding.

```bash

helm uninstall flowise --namespace flowise

gcloud container clusters delete flowise-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command removes the Flowise release.
> This command deletes the entire cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Flowise
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This step will upgrade Flowise to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade flowise flowise/flowise --version 3.0.13 --namespace flowise

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Cordon and drain the node before upgrading.
> Upgrade Flowise to the specified version.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or there may be network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure your cluster has internet access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Flowise application may not be starting correctly due to configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n flowise' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the Flowise deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Flowise service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is running and check the service type and ports.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI agent types and improved UI for agent management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.x, review the migration guide for changes in configuration structure.

resolution.summary: You should see the Flowise UI running at http://localhost:3000 and the pods in the 'flowise' namespace should be in 'Running' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
